title: Tristan Musgrave
content:
People.ContactEmail tmusgrave@mccarthy.ca
Clients value Tristan’s ability to identify and implement practical, commercially-minded solutions to complex issues - keeping procurements and projects on track and within budget.
Tristan Musgrave is a partner in McCarthy Tétrault’s Real Property & Planning, Projects, and Construction Law Groups in Toronto, and co-head of the firm’s national Procurement Group.
A trusted and highly personable advisor to public and private-sector clients alike, he has a broad commercial real estate, infrastructure, and procurement practice, with a particular specialization in complex real estate and infrastructure development and management transactions, including land rights acquisitions (land purchases, leasing, subleasing, easements, and licenses), financing, procurement, stakeholder and regulatory approvals, insurance, construction, and operations matters.
Recognized as one of Canada’s leading infrastructure lawyers, Tristan has advised on many of the country’s most significant infrastructure projects for nearly 20 years. His infrastructure experience spans a wide range of industries, including transportation/transit, healthcare, education, justice, government services, energy, and asset maintenance/operations. He assists clients in developing strategic and practical transaction structures, and brings in-depth experience across virtually all modern contract models, including:
- traditional delivery approaches (design-build, design-bid-build, and construction management);
- bespoke construction contracts and long-term service agreements;
- public-private partnerships (PPP/P3);
- collaborative development models, such as progressive design-build, progressive P3, integrated project delivery (IPD) and alliance agreements; and
- complex ground lease and land development arrangements.
Tristan also has deep experience post-closing in resolving significant infrastructure disputes and efficiently restructuring projects to overcome challenges and deliver successful outcomes.
In his extensive public procurement practice, Tristan advises on the development, structuring, and administration of competitive tender processes, and crafting procurement policies and templates. He regularly supports both procuring entities and bidders throughout intricate procurements, identifying and mitigating fairness risks, negotiating definitive agreements, handling bidder inquiries and complaints, and ensuring compliance with procurement laws and policies (e.g., Ontario’s Broader Public Sector Procurement Directive (BPSD) and Canadian trade treaties).

#### Representative Experience
Some of Tristan's representative experience includes advising:
- The winning bidder on the Federal Government’s High-Frequency (now High-Speed) Rail project in Ontario and Quebec, one of the largest infrastructure projects in Canadian history.
- Infrastructure Ontario, Metrolinx, Waterfront Toronto, hospitals, colleges and other public sector entities on over 20 significant P3 transactions in Ontario, including on: the $13.9 billion Peter Gilgan Mississauga Hospital and Shah Family Hospital for Women and Children Progressive DBFM Project. the Hazel McCallion Line (Hurontario LRT) Light Rail Transit DBFOM Project. the Ontario Court of Justice (New Toronto Courthouse) DBFM Project. the Oakville Trafalgar Memorial Hospital DBFM Project. the Macdonald Block Reconstruction DBFM Project. 17 facility Ontario Provincial Police Modernization DBFM Project. the Pan/Parapan Am Games Athletes' Village DBF Project, and related real estate transactions. the vivaNext Bus Rapid Transit DBF Project. the Sheridan College DBF Project. the Mount Sinai Hospital Phase 3A Redevelopment BF Project.
- the $13.9 billion Peter Gilgan Mississauga Hospital and Shah Family Hospital for Women and Children Progressive DBFM Project.
- the Hazel McCallion Line (Hurontario LRT) Light Rail Transit DBFOM Project.
- the Ontario Court of Justice (New Toronto Courthouse) DBFM Project.
- the Oakville Trafalgar Memorial Hospital DBFM Project.
- the Macdonald Block Reconstruction DBFM Project.
- 17 facility Ontario Provincial Police Modernization DBFM Project.
- the Pan/Parapan Am Games Athletes' Village DBF Project, and related real estate transactions.
- the vivaNext Bus Rapid Transit DBF Project.
- the Sheridan College DBF Project.
- the Mount Sinai Hospital Phase 3A Redevelopment BF Project.
- Infrastructure Ontario and the Ontario Ministry of Transportation on the QEW Garden City Skyway Progressive Design-Build Project.
- Infrastructure Ontario and Metrolinx on the Scarborough Subway Extension and the Yonge North Subway Extension.
- Universities and public hospitals in Ontario with regards to the acquisition, sale, and development of real estate, including by way of ground lease and lease-leaseback models.
- A prominent public sector agency with respect to the development and implementation of a solicitation (procurement) policy for significant real estate development projects.
- The Corporation of Massey Hall and Roy Thomson Hall on the multi-phase revitalization of Toronto’s historic Massey Hall.
- Private sector developers and investors, in high-profile developments, including Cityzen Development Group, Minto, Diamond Corp., and CreateTO.
- Fairmont Hotels & Resorts on strategic property dispositions, including the sale of the landmark Fairmont Newfoundland hotel.
- Artscape on the development of the award-winning Wychwood Barns community arts complex in Toronto (pro bono).

#### Education & Affiliation
Tristan received his BA (Hons.) in Economics from Queen’s University in 2001, his MA in Economics from Queen’s University in 2002, and his BCL/LLB from McGill University in 2005. He was called to the Ontario bar in 2007.
Tristan is a member of the Law Society of Ontario, the Ontario Bar Association, and the Canadian Bar Association. He has also completed the Osgoode Hall Certificate in Public Procurement Law and the Osgoode Hall Certificate in Public Procurement Governance, and has been a lecturer as part of numerous Schulich School of Businesses and Osgood Hall Professional Development programs.
